Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Records To Protect Defendant From Possible Harm; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Feb-16-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JEANNE FUGATE, Esq. of King & Spalding LLP (Tel: 415-318-1200) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JANCE WEBERMAN, Esq. of Jance M. Weberman, APLC (Tel: 213-386-9100) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT JULIA KO's Motion To Seal Records To Protect Defendant From Possible
Harm.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Denied without prejudice. The sealing is not narrowly tailored per CRC 2.550(d) and (e).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/ Victor Da Fonseca. Court Reporter: Paige Hutchinson, CSR #13459, Tel. 951.415.5500, Email: thatcourtreporter@gmail.com. Deputy: Nguyen. (302/RCE)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JEANNE A. FUGATE, Esq. of King & Spalding LLP (Tel: 415-318-1200) for plaintiff;
Atty. LAWRENCE ADAMSKY, Esq. "specially appearing" for atty. Jance M. Weberman, Esq. (Tel: 213-386-9100) for defendant Julia G. Ko.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JULIA KO's Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgment.

The Court notes it did not reeceive a notice of contest from defense counsel but permits argument from counsel for parties.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "motion to set aside default and vacate default judgment" is denied. The same matter was ruled on in the court's November 29, 2023 order on defendant's previous "motion to set aside default and leave to defend." "The name of the motion is not controlling, and, regardless of the name, a motion asking the trial court to decide the same matter previously ruled on is a motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008." (Powell v. County of Orange (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1573, 1577.) A reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500; CCP 1008.) The moving party shall state by affidavit "what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (Id.) Defendant's pending motion papers do not meet these
requirements.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Court Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR No. 13293, Email: sheilapham28@gmail.com, Tel: 415-274-9977 (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-26-2023 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Motion For Court Judgment On Default, And Request For Hearing Date For Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Maria Evangelista; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:51 am and concluding at 9:57 am.

Plantiff Christian Larsen appearing represented by Jeanne Fugate, Esq via zoom video conference.
Defendant Julia Ko not appearing.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

Plaintiff Progressive Foundation submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Declaration of Christina Larsen
Exhibit 2: Declaration of Anna See
Exhibit 3: Declaration of Jeanne Fugate

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Christian Larsen, and against Defendant Julia Ko, as follows:

1. Damages: $0
2. Prejudgment Interest: $0
3. Attorney fees:$0
4. Costs: $4,295.01

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $4,295.01

Additionally, the Court GRANTS Declatory relief as follows: The Court finds that Plaintiff Christian Larsen did not engage in the conduct described in Exhibit A to Plaintiff Christian Larsen's Complaint in this action.

Proposed Judgment signed by the Court. (D505)
